author: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus
book: The Canons of the Two Hundred Holy and Blessed Fathers Who Met at Ephesus.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus.
The Canons of the Two Hundred Holy and Blessed Fathers Who Met at Ephesus.

metadata.author: The Third Ecumenical Council: The Council of Ephesus

title: Canon IV.

verses.book: The Canons of the Two Hundred Holy and Blessed Fathers Who Met at Ephesus.
verses.text: If any of the clergy should fall away, and publicly or privately presume to maintain the doctrines of Nestorius or Celestius, it is declared just by the holy Synod that these also should be deposed.

verses.book: The Canons of the Two Hundred Holy and Blessed Fathers Who Met at Ephesus.
The Canons of the Two Hundred Holy and Blessed Fathers Who Met at Ephesus.
verses.text: If any of the clergy shall consent to Celestine or Nestorius, let them be deposed.
